prose:
Conformational-analysis result for ethylene glycol

Method–provenance block
• Property: relaxed dihedral-scan rotation barrier (ΔE‡) for the HO–CH2–CH2–OH backbone in ethylene glycol; headline value is the difference between the highest and lowest GFN2-xTB single-point energies along the 24-point scan grid.
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) in xtb.
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied geometry (ethylene-glycol.xyz); every scan point was fully relaxed at the same level with the ASE BFGS optimizer (GFN2-xTB//GFN2-xTB).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software/backend: xtb 6.x via chemkit “conformational-analysis” skill (CLI: `scan --method xtb ethylene-glycol.xyz`). Density-fitting intrinsic to GFN2-xTB.
• Scan details: 1 automatically detected rotatable dihedral (atoms 1-2-3-4), 24 steps (15° increment), all 24 points converged. Optimizer criterion fmax ≤ 4.5 × 10⁻³ eV Å⁻¹. Trajectory saved to `/Users/evane/.../agent_call_01_live_dih1_2_3_4.xyz`. Live log was streamed to `<cwd>/conformational-analysis_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out` during execution.
• Convergence & caveats: `converged: true` for all points; no engine warnings; integrity check reports status "ok" and `trustworthy: true`.

Result
Rotation barrier about the O–C–C–O dihedral: 5.6401367 kcal mol⁻¹ (grid maximum – minimum). An interpolated spline fit yields 4.1156583 kcal mol⁻¹, but the grid value is reported as the headline barrier.

No experimental comparison requested. All data and logs are available at the paths above for full reproducibility.
